role: VP for Management and Human Resources Development (2009–2012); VP for Parliamentary Affairs (2012–2013); former IRGC commander
bio: Lotfollah Forouzandeh Dehkordi is an Iranian principlist politician and former [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) commander who served as Vice President for Management and Human Resources Development (2009–2012) and Vice President for Parliamentary Affairs (2012–2013) under [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad). He held command and deputy positions in the IRGC until 1992. Forouzandeh is a co-founder of the Society of Devotees of the Islamic Revolution (Isargaran), a group of former Iran–Iraq War commanders that played a key role in Ahmadinejad's rise to power through the Tehran Municipality and the presidency. In 2024 he served as campaign manager for Tehran mayor Alireza Zakani's presidential bid.

sources.title: Controversy Erupts as Tehran Mayor Appoints Campaign Manager

editorial_notes: Born 1961. Former IRGC commander (until 1992). Co-founder of Isargaran. VP Management 2009-2012, VP Parliamentary Affairs 2012-2013. Campaign manager for Zakani 2024.
